message:
test(packs): kubectl pattern coverage + alphabetize use lists in infra packs

src/packs/kubernetes/kubectl.rs:
- Add a `crate::packs::Severity` import in the test module so tests
  can call `assert_blocks_with_severity`.
- New `kubectl_blocks_each_destructive_pattern` test that walks every
  destructive pattern the kubectl pack ships with and asserts both
  the block decision and the canonical reason fragment:
  - `delete namespace` / `delete ns`           → "namespace"
  - `delete pods --all`                          → "--all"
  - `delete pods --all-namespaces` / `-A`        → "ALL namespaces"
  - `drain` / `cordon`                            → "drain" / "cordon"
  - `taint nodes ... :NoExecute`                 → "NoExecute"
  - `delete deployment` / `delete statefulset`   → "workload"
  - `delete pvc` / `delete pv`                   → "pvc" / "pv"
  - `scale ... --replicas=0`                     → "replicas=0"
  - `delete --force --grace-period=0`            → "force"
  - `apply -f ... --force`                       → "force"
  - `delete -f ./dir/`                           → "directory"
- New `kubectl_blocks_with_correct_severity` test that asserts the
  expected `Severity` value for each high-severity pattern (delete
  namespace etc.).

src/packs/infrastructure/pulumi.rs and terraform.rs:
- Reorder the existing `use` lists so `crate::packs::Severity` comes
  before `crate::packs::test_helpers::*` (rustfmt's preferred
  alphabetical order). No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
